1.description: Term structure of carry risk premia. This figure plots the term structure of carry risk premia. The line labeled with marker is the term structure of average carry log returns (data), the thick line is the model-implied term structure of carry risk premia, the thin lines correspond to the 95% credible interval, and the dashed line is the term structure of average carry log returns implied by the model. The level of stochastic variance is set atvt=1. The data are quarterly over the period 1986Q2 to 2011Q4. Numbers are in percent.

2.description: Shock exposure elasticity: Unrestricted VAR. This figure plots shock exposure elasticities for consumption growth risk (Panel A), inflation risk (Panel B), interest rate risk (Panel C), and variance risk (Panel D). The dashed line is for the basket “Low,” the thin solid line is for the basket “Intermediate,” the line labeled with marker is for the basket “High,” and the thick solid line with vertical bars indicating 95% credible intervals is for the basket “High-Minus-Low.” The level of stochastic variance is set atvt=1. The data are quarterly over the period 1986Q2 to 2011Q4.

3.description: Shock exposure elasticity. Structural VAR. This figure plots shock exposure elasticities for consumption growth risk (Panel A), inflation risk (Panel B), interest rate risk (Panel C), and variance risk (Panel D). The dashed line is for the basket “Low,” the thin solid line is for the basket “Intermediate,” the line labeled with marker is for the basket “High,” and the thick solid line with vertical bars indicating 95% credible intervals is for the basket “High-Minus-Low.” The level of stochastic variance is set atvt=1. The data are quarterly over the period 1986Q2 to 2011Q4.

4.description: Shock price elasticity. This figure plots shock price elasticities for consumption growth risk (Panel A), inflation risk (Panel B), interest rate risk (Panel C), and variance risk (Panel D). In Panels A, B, and C shock price elasticity is the same for the currency baskets. In Panel D, shock price elasticity is basket specific. The dashed line is for the basket “Low,” the thin solid line is for the basket “Intermediate,” the line labeled with marker is for the basket “High,” and the thick solid line with vertical bars indicating 95% credible intervals is for the basket “High-Minus-Low.” The level of stochastic variance is set atvt=1. The data are quarterly over the period 1986Q2 to 2011Q4.

5.description: Impact of the structural shocks on future consumption growth. This figure plots the horizon-dependent response of future expected one-period consumption growthlogEtgt+i−1,t+ito the individual shocks of unit standard deviation, whereiranges from one to 40 quarters. The line labeled with marker is for the consumption growth shock. The thin solid line is for the inflation shock. The thick solid grey line is for the long-run growth shock. The dashed line is for the variance shock. The level of stochastic variance is set atvt=1. The data are quarterly over the period 1947Q2 to 2011Q4.

6.description: Shock exposure elasticity with and without restrictions. The figure plots shock exposure elasticity for consumption growth risk, inflation risk, interest rate risk, and variance risk. The dashed grey lines indicate elasticities in the unrestricted case. The black line labeled with marker is the mean path and the thin solid black lines are the 95% credible intervals of the corresponding shock exposure elasticities in the case in which the cross-equation restrictions are imposed. The level of stochastic variance is set atvt=1. The data are quarterly over the period 1947Q2 to 2015Q4.

7.description: Stochastic variance factor. The figure plots a common stochastic variance factor of consumption growth, inflation, nominal interest rate, and exchange rate growth. The thick line is the estimated mean path. The thin lines corresponds to the 95% credible interval. The data are quarterly from 1947Q2 to 2011Q4. Grey bars indicate the NBER recessions.
